commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR hardens agent runtime tool-schema projection, doctor diagnostics, OpenAI strict-schema diagnostics, quarantine logging, and effective inventory handling for unreadable or unsupported tool schemas.

PR surface: Source +468, Tests +399. Total +867 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main reads tool rows, names, and parameters directly during projection/doctor/quarantine paths, so throwing getters can abort before diagnostics.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A focused repair can remove the duplicate inventory filter, but maintainers still need to accept the compatibility behavior and rerun broad proof before merge.

target.title: fix(agents): harden runtime tool schema diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary

- Quarantines active runtime tools whose input schema cannot safely reach provider/model runtime projection, instead of letting one bad tool crash assistant turn setup before content.
- Moves unreadable/non-serializable tool filtering ahead of provider normalization in live embedded runs and doctor checks, while preserving later projection checks for provider-repairable schemas.
- Extends doctor, OpenAI strict-schema diagnostics, quarantine logging, and effective inventory handling so bad tool rows/schemas report clearly without dropping healthy sibling tools.

## Linked context

Related #87141

This is the critical runtime/doctor slice from the larger fuzzing branch. Broader fixture scrub lives in #88376; provider catalog isolation lives in #88351.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: an unsupported or unreadable active tool schema is filtered/quarantined before model session creation, and doctor/inventory diagnostics can surface the blocker instead of crashing or silently skipping it.
- Real environment tested: final rebased local focused Vitest wrapper across runtime projection, doctor checks, and live embedded attempt setup; AWS Crabbox changed gate on the pushed patch surface.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts`
  - `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
  - private-name/private-path branch scrub
  - `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
  - `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`
- Evidence after fix: final rebased focused Vitest passed 3 routed shards / 77 tests at head `8851e717260d`; oxfmt passed; diff check passed; scrub returned no matches; autoreview was clean.
- Observed result after fix: unreadable tool rows, unreadable names/parameters, and non-serializable parameter schemas are removed before provider normalization; provider-repairable raw schemas still reach provider normalization; post-normalization unsupported schemas are quarantined before model session creation; healthy sibling tools remain available.
- What was not tested: a live packaged third-party plugin. Coverage uses synthetic `fuzzplugin`/`mockplugin` fixtures and the same runtime projection path used by assistant setup.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: AWS Crabbox `run_460674696d5f` on lease `cbx_5ee2eeec2ac7` ran `pnpm check:changed` but failed in current-main core typecheck at untouched `src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts:844` and `:891`; the same failure appeared on the separate trusted-policy branch gate, so this PR's broad changed gate is blocked by main drift. A follow-up clean-main proof attempt `run_04195f075abc` could not start because the lease had already stopped.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- private-name/private-path branch scrub
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` (blocked by unrelated current-main type errors above)

Regression coverage added:

- runtime projection filtering for unreadable tool descriptors and rows
- provider-normalizable prefiltering that preserves provider-repairable schemas
- doctor runtime schema findings for unreadable active tool rows without throwing
- live embedded attempt quarantine before provider normalization and model session creation
- quarantine logging that does not re-read unreadable rows
- effective inventory quarantine before provider normalization

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Bad active runtime tool schemas are reported/quarantined instead of crashing assistant turn setup.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Tool execution availability now fails around the bad tool instead of failing the whole assistant turn.

What is the highest-risk area?

Runtime compatibility for plugins/tools with malformed schema metadata.

How is that risk mitigated?

The pre-normalization filter only removes unreadable rows/fields and non-serializable schemas. Provider-repairable schemas still reach provider normalization, and the existing post-normalization runtime projection quarantines schemas the model runtime still cannot accept.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Draft PR for maintainer review and CI. It should land after #88376 so fixture scrub noise is already gone.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

GitHub CI, maintainer review, and broad changed-gate rerun after the current-main auth-profile typecheck drift is fixed.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

ClawSweeper/local review findings on doctor unreadable-row handling, pre-normalization provider repair false positives, live attempt normalization ordering, earlier unsafe name reads, and mutable normalization input were all addressed before this push.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
